content.subtitle: What is a conditional sentence?
items: Some sentences with the word **if** are called conditional sentences.
A conditional sentence has two parts: the situation (if-clause) and the result.
There are different types of conditional sentences depending on the situation.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: When we start the sentence with **if**, we separate the situation and result with a comma
✓ **If you join a gym, I'll join too.**
When we start with the result clause, we usually do not use a comma
✓ **I'll join too if you join a gym.**

content.subtitle: Use
rows: If people eat too much, they often get fat.

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: if + present simple, will + bare infinitive

content.subtitle: Use
rows: Real or likely situations in the present or future and their results
If you take these pills, you'll start to feel better very soon.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We can use other modals instead of **will**, depending on meaning.
items: **If you get some rest, you might feel better tomorrow.**
We can use an imperative instead of **will** to give instructions: **If you don't feel well, go home!**

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: if + past simple, would + bare infinitive

content.subtitle: Use
rows: Impossible or unlikely situations in the present or future and their results
If my legs were longer, I would be a much faster runner!

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We can also use the second conditional to give advice with **If I were you ...** or **If I was you ...**.
items: **If I were you, I'd eat less chocolate.** (more formal)
**If I was you, I'd eat less chocolate.** (more informal)

sections.title: A: Zero conditional
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct present simple form.

items.sentence: If you ...... (heat) water to 100°C, it ...... (boil).
If plants ...... (not get) enough light, they ...... (not grow) well.
If people ...... (eat) too much sugar, they often ...... (feel) tired later.
If I ...... (drink) coffee late, I ...... (not sleep) well.
If we ...... (mix) blue and yellow, we ...... (get) green.
If students ...... (not revise), they usually ...... (make) more mistakes.

sections.title: B: First conditional
sections.instructions: Complete the first conditional sentences with the correct verb forms.

items.sentence: If it ...... (rain), we ...... (stay) at home.
If you ...... (study) tonight, you ...... (do) better in the test.
If I ...... (see) Anna, I ...... (tell) her the news.
If they ...... (leave) now, they ...... (arrive) on time.
If we ...... (not hurry), we ...... (miss) the bus.
If you ...... (not feel) well tomorrow, ...... (call) the doctor.

sections.title: C: Second conditional
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct second conditional form.

items.sentence: If I ...... (have) more free time, I ...... (learn) to play the piano.
If she ...... (be) taller, she ...... (play) basketball for the school team.
If we ...... (live) near the sea, we ...... (go) swimming every day.
If he ...... (not spend) so much money, he ...... (save) more.
If they ...... (know) the answer, they ...... (tell) us.
If I ...... (be) you, I ...... (take) that job.

sections.title: D: Choose the correct conditional
sections.instructions: Circle the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentence: If you **press / pressed** this button, the machine **starts / would start**.
If he **studies / studied** more, he **passes / will pass** the exam.
If I **am / were** rich, I **buy / would buy** a house in the mountains.
If we **don't water / didn't water** the plants, they **die / will die**.
If they **leave / left** now, they **arrive / will arrive** before eight.
If she **was / were** more confident, she **applies / would apply** for that role.

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I don't have enough money, so I can't buy a new laptop. **WOULD**
If I had enough money, I ...... a new laptop.

Take this medicine and you'll feel better. **IF**
...... this medicine, you'll feel better.

People get cold when the temperature drops. **IF**
If the temperature drops, people ...... cold.

You don't hurry, so you'll miss the train. **MISS**
If you don't hurry, you ...... the train.

I'm not you, so I don't complain so much. **WERE**
If I ...... you, I wouldn't complain so much.

When she is nervous, she makes mistakes. **IF**
If she ...... nervous, she makes mistakes.
